profile.name: shashwat sharma
profile.bio: Learning the way to learn new things

prs.title: Auth debounce fix
prs.body:
# Issue

- Username availability check was bypassing the debounce logic and sending API requests on every keystroke.
- Both username and email availability APIs were being triggered regardless of the active signup step.

# Fix

- Split the shared `useEffect` into separate effects for:
  - Step 1: Email availability check
  - Step 4: Username availability check
- Updated the effects to call the correct handlers:
  - `handleEmailAvailabilityCheck`
  - `handleUsernameAvailabilityCheck`
- Ensured API calls are only triggered on their respective signup steps and respect the debounce behavior.

# Files Changed

## Modified
- `AUTH.tsx`

## Added
- `.env`
  - Added `MYSQL_PASS`

prs.title: docs(router): update navigation event example to use event.code

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Milestone for triaged issues that have not yet been scheduled for resolution.

prs.body:
Update the code sample to use `event.code` instead of `event.reason` for cancellation checks, while preserving `event.reason` in the console.warn line. This aligns the example with the NavigationCancellationCode enum and prevents confusion.



## PR Checklist
Please check if your PR fulfills the following requirements:

## What is the new behavior?


## Does this PR introduce a breaking change?

- [ ] Yes
- [ ] No





The previous example incorrectly compared event.code. Updated to use event.reason
to match the current NavigationCancel API. This fixes the type errors that were
caused by the old example.

Closes #65232
Closes #65235

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Milestone for triaged issues that have not yet been scheduled for resolution.

prs.body:
docs: update navigation event example to use event.code

Update the code sample to use `event.code` instead of `event.reason` as
requested in issue #65232. This aligns the example with the updated API and
prevents confusion for developers using the NavigationCancellationCode enum.

The existing use of `event.reason` in the console.warn example is preserved
because it is the correct and intentional usage for that specific case.

Fixes #65232

prs.title: Rename notes
prs.body:
Initially, when you save a note, the first word of the content is automatically used as the note title.
Now, with the rename feature, you can change the title to anything you like — similar to how Google Docs allows renaming documents.

prs.title: added_export_notes_functionality
prs.body: added some ui improvement and feature to download notes

prs.title: notes update
prs.body: Previously, the notes were being stored in the browser's localStorage, which is reset every time the extension is reloaded or the WebView is refreshed. To solve this, we switched to using VS Code’s globalState, which persists data in VS Code’s internal memory, ensuring that notes remain saved even after the extension is closed or reloaded.

prs.title: added folder
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added search functionality to filter saved notes by content.
  * Introduced dropdown menus for each note, allowing downloads in plain text, markdown, or PDF formats.

* **Enhancements**
  * Improved UI/UX for notes display with card layouts, dropdowns, and enhanced styling, including dark mode support.
  * Notes can now be downloaded in multiple formats directly from the interface.
  * Warning and success messages now include emojis for better feedback.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Notes are now trimmed of whitespace before saving.
  * Clearing all notes provides immediate user feedback.

* **Refactor**
  * Simplified and unified styling across components by replacing inline styles with CSS classes and structured layouts.
  * Improved local storage handling and error messages for better reliability.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Download feature
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Enhanced Saved Notes with search, dropdown menu for each note, and multi-format downloads (TXT, MD, PDF).
  * Added new styles for the note-taking component and improved Saved Notes UI with card-based layout and dark mode support.
  * Integrated a File Explorer section into the dashboard.

* **Improvements**
  * Note content is now trimmed before saving, and users receive feedback when all notes are cleared.
  * Search reset functionality added for smoother navigation.
  * Improved conditional query handling for integrated tools.
  * Enhanced error and warning messages for better user feedback.

* **Refactor & Style**
  * Migrated NoteTaker component to use CSS classes instead of inline styles.
  * Reformatted and cleaned up code for consistency and maintainability.
  * Improved accessibility and layout of UI components.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Fixed dropdown menu behavior in Saved Notes to close on outside clicks and after pin toggling.

* **Chores**
  * Updated formatting and indentation in configuration and code files for consistency.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: update tree.jsx and dynamic search
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added a "File Explorer" section with a tree view to the dashboard.
  * Replaced the static developer tip with a dedicated DevTip component.

* **Enhancements**
  * Improved search reset functionality; back button now fully clears search state.
  * JsonValidator and StackOverflow tools now display results only when relevant triggers are detected.

* **Style**
  * Updated StackOverflow and JsonValidator tools to remove emojis from UI elements for a cleaner look.
  * Adjusted input and container styles for improved visual presentation.
  * Shortened the search input placeholder text.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Tree view
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added an interactive folder tree view that dynamically updates and allows expand/collapse, file copying, and integration with VS Code alerts.
  * Introduced a command-driven search interface with trigger-based commands (e.g., @stack, @json, @notes) and autocomplete suggestions.
  * Stack Overflow and JSON Validator tools now accept contextual queries from the search interface.

* **Enhancements**
  * Stack Overflow tool shows top results as hover tooltips in the code editor.
  * Improved UI styling and consistency for search, suggestions, and tool views.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Enhanced error handling for Stack Overflow API responses.

* **Chores**
  * Added new command and keybinding for showing the folder tree in the terminal.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: add dynamic search feature
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Introduced an advanced search input supporting trigger-based commands (e.g., "@stack", "@json", "@notes") with autocomplete suggestions and visual highlighting.
  * Selecting a trigger switches the main view and passes the relevant query to Stack Overflow or JSON Validator tools.

* **Enhancements**
  * Improved styling for the search input and autocomplete dropdown, including transparent text highlights and better suggestion visibility.
  * Stack Overflow and JSON Validator components now accept and initialize with external queries for a smoother workflow.
  * Refined UI styling and layout details in the Stack Overflow component for improved readability and user experience.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Enhanced input handling and error messaging in the Stack Overflow component for greater clarity and reliability.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
